recom: Keep your usual controller plan and check the forecast again later.
Keep your quick-relief inhaler available and reduce exposure to the main driver.
Follow your asthma action plan today and modify outdoor activity or exposure.
Shift activities to a cleaner or safer indoor setting and monitor closely today.
Treat today as a high-risk exposure day: minimize exposure, keep rescue medication ready, and use your action plan promptly if symptoms begin or worsen.
Make outdoor activity shorter and less intense.
Take more breaks outside and keep your quick-relief inhaler handy.
Move long or intense activity indoors or reschedule it.
If there is high pollen in your area, stay indoors, close doors and windows, and keep your reliever with you.
Use filtered or air-conditioned air if available.
Stay cool, drink water regularly, and reduce time in heat.
Cover your nose and mouth in cold air and warm up before exertion.
Reduce indoor humidity and avoid damp or moldy spaces.
Use your pre-exercise treatment plan and warm up before activity.
When pollen exposure is likely, keep windows closed and change clothes after being outside.
